target.query:
from(bucket: "netmon")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "netmon_event" and r._field == "description")
  |> keep(columns: ["_time", "_value", "category", "source"])
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({ r with text: r._value, tags: r.category + "," + r.source }))

from(bucket: "netmon")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "ping_incident" and r._field == "rtt_max")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r.synthetic != "true")
  |> keep(columns: ["_time", "_value", "target_name", "type"])
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({ r with text: r.target_name + " " + r.type + " (" + string(v: r._value) + "ms)", tags: r.type }))

description: Router-side telemetry, scoped to what the Omada CLI actually exposes on this firmware (read-only). See docs/omada-cli.md for the full list of probes and dead-ends.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "netmon")
  |> range(start: -10m)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "router_info" and r._field == "firmware")
  |> last()

panels.title: Uptime
panels.description: Router uptime in seconds. A sudden drop = the router rebooted. Threshold red if <5 min.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "netmon")
  |> range(start: -10m)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "router_info" and r._field == "uptime_seconds")
  |> last()

panels.title: Active LAN devices
panels.description: Devices currently in the router's ARP table on vlan1. Includes infrastructure (the 2 EAPs + the Pi), so subtract 3 for the human-client count. Polled from `show arp` once per cycle.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "netmon")
  |> range(start: -10m)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "router_lan" and r._field == "total_devices")
  |> last()

from(bucket: "netmon")
  |> range(start: -5m)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "router_wan" and r._field == "port1_up")
  |> last()

from(bucket: "netmon")
  |> range(start: -5m)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "router_wan" and r._field == "port2_up")
  |> last()

from(bucket: "netmon")
  |> range(start: -5m)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "router_wan" and r._field == "port3_up")
  |> last()

panels.title: Active LAN devices over time
panels.description: Devices in the router's ARP table on vlan1 over time. Peaks correlate with workday occupancy. Drops to ~3 = only infrastructure (overnight). Useful to correlate incident density with people present.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "netmon")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "router_lan" and r._field == "total_devices")
  |> aggregateWindow(every: v.windowPeriod, fn: max, createEmpty: false)

panels.title: WAN gateway L2 reachability
panels.description: 1 = the ISP's upstream gateway answered ARP within the last cycle (= link up at L2). Complements port UP/DOWN: a port can be UP but the upstream ISP gateway unreachable. vlan0 = Movistar (WAN2), vlan4093 = Orange (WAN3).

targets.query:
from(bucket: "netmon")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "router_wan_l2" and r._field == "gateway_up")
  |> aggregateWindow(every: v.windowPeriod, fn: min, createEmpty: false)

panels.title: WAN port UP/DOWN history
panels.description: L1/L2 status from `show interface switchport N`. Each line steps between 0 (DOWN) and 1 (UP) so drops are obvious. Cross-reference with the L2 reachability panel above.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "netmon")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "router_wan" and (r._field == "port1_up" or r._field == "port2_up" or r._field == "port3_up"))
  |> aggregateWindow(every: v.windowPeriod, fn: min, createEmpty: false)

panels.title: Router-side ping during incidents (Pi-vs-router attribution)
panels.description: When ping_monitor detects an incident from the Pi side, wan_ping.py SSH's into the router and pings the same target. If the router's RTT is normal while the Pi's was high, the problem is between Pi and router (WiFi/mesh). If both are high, the problem is upstream of the router. Empty stretches = no incidents (good).

targets.query:
from(bucket: "netmon")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "router_ping" and r._field == "rtt_avg")
  |> group(columns: ["target"])
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({ r with _field: "Router \u2192 " + r.target }))

from(bucket: "netmon")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "ping" and r._field == "rtt_avg" and (r.target_name == "dns_google" or r.target_name == "dns_cloudflare"))
  |> aggregateWindow(every: v.windowPeriod, fn: mean, createEmpty: false)
  |> group(columns: ["target_name"])
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({ r with _field: "Pi \u2192 " + r.target_name }))

panels.title: WAN port state changes (events)
panels.description: Discrete events when a port flips UP↔DOWN. Empty = no flaps in window (boring is good).

targets.query:
from(bucket: "netmon")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "wan_event" and r._field == "message")
  |> sort(columns: ["_time"], desc: true)
  |> limit(n: 50)
  |> keep(columns: ["_time", "_value", "port", "type"])

panels.title: Notes
panels.description: What this dashboard can and cannot show, given the firmware's constraints.

options.content:
**This dashboard is intentionally slim.** The Omada CLI on the ER706W (cloud-managed) exposes essentially no traffic counters, no CPU/memory/temperature, no per-WAN bandwidth metrics, and no syslog. SNMP is disabled and cannot be enabled (firmware quirk — `(config)#` mode is a stub). The Cloud-Based Controller does not expose an Open API for this product line (only the self-hosted Software Controller does, since v5.12).

**What we DO have here:**
- WAN port L1/L2 status (UP/DOWN per port; ISP gateway ARP reachability)
- Uptime + firmware version (reboot detection)
- ARP-derived count of active LAN devices (proxy for workday occupancy)
- Router-side ping during incidents (Pi-vs-router attribution)

**Traffic / bandwidth measurement lives elsewhere:** the iperf3 collector (Bandwidth dashboard) and ping_monitor at 1 Hz to 5 internet targets (Connectivity dashboard) provide load-balancer hash signals indirectly.

For the full mapping of Omada CLI capabilities and dead-ends, see `docs/omada-cli.md` in the repo.

title: Router & WAN
